title: Rent Payment Assistance * Disaster/Emergency Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Rent Payment Assistance * Disaster/Emergency Issues 1 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Disaster/Emergency Issues Domestic Violence Issues Domestic Violence Issues * African American Community Domestic Violence Issues * LGBTQ2+ Individuals Families with Children Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Veterans Homelessness Issues LGBTQ2+ Individuals Medicaid Recipients Mental Health Issues Older Adults Pregnant Individuals Sexual Assault Issues Slavic Community Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES HOUSING SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists with rent and utility assistance (electric, gas, heating fuel, sewer, trash, or water), down payment for a home, or financial counseling, which may include household budgeting, eviction protection, and legal aid. Rent assistance participants must pay 30% of their income towards rent. Assistance is available for 24 months or until the program’s funding is entirely spent. Eligibility: -Household affected by the wildfires and straight-line winds of 2020, which include the 242, Almeda, Archie Creek, Beachie Creek, Echo Mountain, Holiday Farm, Lionshead, Riverside, and South Obenchain fires -At the time of the disasters, the applicant lived in an impacted county -Current household income at or below 120% of Area Medium Income (AMI) -Rent/Utility Assistance: Monthly housing costs exceed 30% of applicant's household’s adjusted gross monthly income or housing is not decent, safe, or sanitary and the cost of housing that meets occupancy standards would exceed 30% of household's income Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 250-0938 Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
